name: automake
title: automake
summary: Tool for generating GNU Standards-compliant Makefiles
description:
Tool for generating GNU Standards-compliant Makefiles
Automake is a tool for automatically generating `Makefile.in's from
files called `Makefile.am'.
.
The goal of Automake is to remove the burden of Makefile maintenance
from the back of the individual GNU maintainer (and put it on the back
of the Automake maintainer).
.
The `Makefile.am' is basically a series of `make' macro definitions
(with rules being thrown in occasionally).  The generated
`Makefile.in's are compliant with the GNU Makefile standards.

arguments.description: pretend to, but do not actually update any file
arguments.raw: --dry-run  pretend to, but do not actually update any file

arguments.description: add directory to search list for .m4 files
arguments.raw: -I  add directory to search list for .m4 files

arguments.description: copy third-party files to the first -I directory
arguments.raw: --install  copy third-party files to the first -I directory

arguments.description: print name of directory holding system-wide third-party m4 files, then exit
arguments.raw: --print-ac-dir  print name of directory holding system-wide third-party m4 files, then exit

commands.name: automake-1.18
commands.description: Generate Makefile.in files for configure from Makefile.am

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use aclocal-1.18 because Tool for generating GNU Standards-compliant Makefiles.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use aclocal-1.18 as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair aclocal-1.18 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `aclocal-1.18 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
